message:
feat(scheduler): implement async task execution with work-stealing waker

Replaces the placeholder task execution logic in the worker with a
complete async runtime implementation that properly polls futures and
handles wake notifications.

New Worker::execute implementation:
- Retrieves stored future from RuntimeState via remove_stored_future
- Marks task as running and clears previous wake state
- Creates a WorkStealingWaker that implements std::task::Wake
- Establishes task context via Cx::set_current for the duration
- Polls the future with proper waker context

Poll::Ready handling:
- Marks task as completed with Outcome::Ok(())
- Notifies dependent tasks via state.task_completed()
- Schedules any unblocked waiters to the global queue

Poll::Pending handling:
- Re-stores the future in RuntimeState for later polling

New WorkStealingWaker struct:
- Holds task_id, wake_state, global queue reference, and parker
- schedule(): Atomically notifies wake_state and pushes to global queue
- Implements Wake trait for both wake() and wake_by_ref()
- Unparks workers to ensure timely processing of newly-ready tasks

Test cleanup:
- Renames worker1 to thief_worker for clarity in steal test

This completes the core execution loop for the work-stealing scheduler,
enabling proper async/await execution with cooperative wake semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
